name: card_image_lt_of_exists_distinct_same_image
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.FiniteSet

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem card_image_lt_of_exists_distinct_same_image
    {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq β]
    (s : Finset α) (f : α → β) {a b : α}
    (ha : a ∈ s) (hb : b ∈ s) (hab : a ≠ b) (hfab : f a = f b) :
    (s.image f).card < s.card := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set induction bookkeeping for Mochizuki Lemma 2.2: if a map identifies two distinct elements of a finite set, the image has smaller cardinality. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_distinct_same_image_of_maps_to_smaller

formal_statement:
theorem exists_distinct_same_image_of_maps_to_smaller
    {α β : Type*}
    (s : Finset α) (t : Finset β) (f : α → β)
    (hcard : t.card < s.card)
    (hmap : ∀ a ∈ s, f a ∈ t) :
    ∃ a ∈ s, ∃ b ∈ s, a ≠ b ∧ f a = f b := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set induction bookkeeping for Mochizuki Lemma 2.2: any map from a finite set into a strictly smaller finite target set identifies two source points. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: card_image_lt_of_subset_with_smaller_subimage
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.FiniteSet

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem card_image_lt_of_subset_with_smaller_subimage
    {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq β]
    (s u : Finset α) (f : α → β)
    (hu : u ⊆ s)
    (hcard : (u.image f).card < u.card) :
    (s.image f).card < s.card := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set induction bookkeeping for Mochizuki Lemma 2.2: if a subset has smaller image cardinality, then the ambient finite set has smaller image cardinality. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: card_image_lt_of_subset_card_four_image_le_three
header:

    {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq β]

    (hu : u ⊆ s)
    (hucard : u.card = 4)
    (himage : (u.image f).card ≤ 3) :
    (s.image f).card < s.card := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set induction bookkeeping for Mochizuki Lemma 2.2: four distinguished source points with at most three image values force a strict image-cardinality drop. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: finset_complex_inf_norm_sub_pos
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem finset_complex_inf_norm_sub_pos
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (hS : S.Nonempty) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    0 < S.inf' hS (fun α : ℂ => ‖α - β‖) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Complex finite-set source layer: the nearest-point distance from β to a finite set not containing β is strictly positive. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_complex_point_nearby_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_complex_point_nearby_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, lam ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S, C * ‖β - lam‖ ≤ ‖α - lam‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 analytic source layer: choose a pole lam near β so every point of the finite set is C times farther from lam than β is. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, lam ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 reciprocal-map source layer: after choosing a pole lam, the map z ↦ 1/(z-lam) separates β from the finite set by factor C. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S,
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
            (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ α) ≠
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 projective reciprocal-map source layer: choose a pole so β maps away from 0 and infinity, the finite set maps away from infinity, and the affine norm separation estimate holds. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_rational_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_rational_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℚ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : (β : ℂ) ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℚ,
      (lam : ℂ) ≠ (β : ℂ) ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, (lam : ℂ) ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) (β : ℂ)‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 rational refinement: if β is rational, choose the reciprocal-map pole lam rational while keeping the same finite-set separation estimate. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_projective_rational_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_projective_rational_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℚ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : (β : ℂ) ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℚ,
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ)
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ (β : ℂ)) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ)
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ (β : ℂ)) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S,
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ)
            (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ α) ≠
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) (β : ℂ)‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 rational projective reciprocal-map source layer: if β is rational, choose a rational pole so β maps away from 0 and infinity, the finite set maps away from infinity, and the affine norm separation estimate holds. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_projective_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_projective_finset
    (S : Finset (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.P1 ℂ)) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ)
    (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ ∧
      (∀ p ∈ S,
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam p ≠
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ) ∧
      ∀ α : ℂ, HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ α ∈ S →
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 projective finite-set source layer: for a finite subset of P1(C) and affine β outside it, choose a reciprocal translate sending β away from 0 and infinity, every projective point of S away from infinity, and satisfying the affine norm estimate on affine members. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_projective_rational_reciprocalTranslate_separating_projective_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_projective_rational_reciprocalTranslate_separating_projective_finset
    (S : Finset (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.P1 ℂ)) (β : ℚ) (C : ℝ)
    (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ (β : ℂ) ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℚ,
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ)
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ (β : ℂ)) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ)
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ (β : ℂ)) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ ∧
      (∀ p ∈ S,
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ (lam : ℂ) p ≠
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ) ∧
      ∀ α : ℂ, HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ α ∈ S →
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) (β : ℂ)‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 rational projective finite-set source layer: if β is rational, choose a rational reciprocal-translate pole for a finite subset of P1(C), allowing infinity in the finite set. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

      Module.finrank K L := by

      X.presheaf.germ (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) x hx (f.app U y) := by

    (K : Type u) [CommRing K] [IsDomain K]

    (map : Φ → X → _root_.ProjectiveSpectrum (grading K))

      ∀ {S T : Set X}, S.Finite → T.Finite → Disjoint S T →
        ∃ φ : Φ, (∀ x ∈ S, map φ x ∈ markedPointSet K) ∧
          ∀ x ∈ T, map φ x ∉ markedPointSet K)

    ∃ φ : Φ,

    (hT : T.Finite) (hTsub : T ⊆ V) :

      IsOpen ((markedSchemeNoncriticalExistence K X Φ schemeMap continuous_schemeMap
        exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.belyiOpen φ) ∧
        T ⊆ ((markedSchemeNoncriticalExistence K X Φ schemeMap continuous_schemeMap
          exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.belyiOpen φ) ∧
          ((markedSchemeNoncriticalExistence K X Φ schemeMap continuous_schemeMap
            exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.belyiOpen φ) ⊆ V := by
informal_prefix: /-- Scheme-marked Belyi layer: for an open with finite complement containing a finite set, the `P1 K` target-carrier interface gives a Belyi open containing that set and contained in the open. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: scheme_marked_belyi_markedSchemeNoncritical_pointwise_cover_complement

theorem scheme_marked_belyi_markedSchemeNoncritical_pointwise_cover_complement
    (K : Type u) [CommRing K] [IsDomain K]
    (X : Type v) [TopologicalSpace X] (Φ : Type w) (κ : Type z)
    (schemeMap : Φ → X → P1 K)
    (continuous_schemeMap : ∀ φ, Continuous (schemeMap φ))
    (exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint :
      ∀ {S T : Set X}, S.Finite → T.Finite → Disjoint S T →
        ∃ φ : Φ, (∀ x ∈ S, schemeMap φ x ∈ markedSchemePointSet K) ∧
          ∀ x ∈ T, schemeMap φ x ∉ markedSchemePointSet K)
    [Finite κ] {S : Set X} (hS : S.Finite)
    (x : κ → {x : X // x ∉ S}) :
    ∃ φ : Φ,
      (markedSchemeNoncriticalExistence K X Φ schemeMap continuous_schemeMap
        exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.sendsSetToBranch S φ ∧
        ∀ i, schemeMap φ (x i).1 ∉ markedSchemePointSet K := by
informal_prefix: /-- Scheme-marked Belyi layer: finite-set existence gives the pointwise tuple-cover hypothesis over a complement for maps targeting the scheme carrier `P1 K`. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: scheme_marked_belyi_markedSchemeNoncritical_finite_subcover_on_complement
header:

theorem scheme_marked_belyi_markedSchemeNoncritical_finite_subcover_on_complement
    (K : Type u) [CommRing K] [IsDomain K]
    (X : Type v) [TopologicalSpace X] (Φ : Type w) (κ : Type z)
    (schemeMap : Φ → X → P1 K)
    (continuous_schemeMap : ∀ φ, Continuous (schemeMap φ))
    (exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint :
      ∀ {S T : Set X}, S.Finite → T.Finite → Disjoint S T →
        ∃ φ : Φ, (∀ x ∈ S, schemeMap φ x ∈ markedSchemePointSet K) ∧
          ∀ x ∈ T, schemeMap φ x ∉ markedSchemePointSet K)
    [Finite κ] [T1Space (P1 K)]
    {S : Set X} (hS : S.Finite) [CompactSpace (κ → {x : X // x ∉ S})] :
    ∃ t : Finset {φ : Φ //
        (markedSchemeNoncriticalExistence K X Φ schemeMap continuous_schemeMap
          exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.sendsSetToBranch S φ},
      (⋃ φ ∈ t,

            exists_scheme_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.complementCoverData S).tupleAvoidSet
              (κ := κ) φ) =
        (Set.univ : Set (κ → {x : X // x ∉ S})) := by

          (∀ x ∈ S, B.schemePointMap F P x ≠ B.schemeTargetPoint F P) ∧

              Polynomial.aeval x P.polynomial.derivative ≠ 0 := by

    (F : Type u) (K : Type v) [Field F] [Field K] [Algebra F K]
    [IsAlgClosed K]
    {S : Set K} (hS : S.Finite)
    (p : F[X]) (hpder : p.derivative ≠ 0) :
    ∃ β : K, ∃ P : P1PolynomialSeparationStep F K S β,
      P.polynomial = p ∧
        concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K P.polynomial β ∉
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.SchemeProjectiveLine.markedSchemePointSet K ∧
          (∀ x ∈ S, concreteSchemePolynomialPointMap F K P.polynomial x ≠
            concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K P.polynomial β) ∧
            ∀ x : K, concreteSchemePolynomialPointMap F K P.polynomial x =
              concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K P.polynomial β →
              Polynomial.aeval x P.polynomial.derivative ≠ 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Concrete polynomial scheme-separation layer: after constructing the scheme point bridge, the selected affine scheme target avoids the marked triple, separates the finite input set, and has only noncritical polynomial preimages. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: belyi_reduction_p1ReductionStep_ofBadValues_bad
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.BelyiReduction

open AlgebraicGeometry
open HilbertTest.SourceStack.BelyiReduction
open HilbertTest.SourceStack.SchemeBelyi
open HilbertTest.SourceStack.SchemeMarkedBelyi
open HilbertTest.SourceStack.SchemeProjectiveLine

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset

universe u
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_reduction_p1ReductionStep_ofBadValues_bad
    (K : Type u) [Field K] {C : Scheme.{u}}
    {hmarkedOpen : IsOpen (markedSchemePointSet K)ᶜ} {S T : Set C}
    (hS : S.Finite)
    (badValues : Set (P1 K)) (hbad : badValues.Finite)
    (aux : C ⟶ P1 K)
    (p1Map : FiniteBelyiMap (markedBelyiTarget K hmarkedOpen) (P1 K))
    (composed : FiniteBelyiMap (markedBelyiTarget K hmarkedOpen) C)
    (composed_base_eq :
      ∀ x : C, composed.hom.base x = p1Map.hom.base (aux.base x))
    (p1Map_maps_bad_to_marked :
      ∀ y ∈ reductionBadSet aux S badValues,
        p1Map.hom.base y ∈ markedSchemePointSet K)
    (targetPoint : P1 K)
    (maps_T_to_target : ∀ x ∈ T, aux.base x = targetPoint)
    (p1Map_target_avoids_marked :
      p1Map.hom.base targetPoint ∉ markedSchemePointSet K) :
    (P1ReductionStep.ofBadValues
      (K := K) (C := C) (hmarkedOpen := hmarkedOpen) (S := S) (T := T)
      hS badValues hbad aux p1Map composed composed_base_eq
      p1Map_maps_bad_to_marked targetPoint maps_T_to_target
      p1Map_target_avoids_marked).bad =
        reductionBadSet aux S badValues := by
informal_prefix: /-- Belyi reduction layer: the constructor from finite bad values uses the standard bad target set `aux(S) ∪ badValues`. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

    (hS : S.Finite) (badValues : Set (P1 K)) (hbad : badValues.Finite)
    (aux : C ⟶ P1 K) [IsFinite aux] [IsDominant aux]
    (p1Map : FiniteBelyiMap (markedBelyiTarget K hmarkedOpen) (P1 K))
    (hcomposedEtale : IsEtale ((aux ≫ p1Map.hom) ∣_ (markedBranchOpen K hmarkedOpen)))
    (p1Map_maps_bad_to_marked :
      ∀ y ∈ reductionBadSet aux S badValues,
        p1Map.hom.base y ∈ markedSchemePointSet K)
    (targetPoint : P1 K)
    (maps_T_to_target : ∀ x ∈ T, aux.base x = targetPoint)
    (p1Map_target_avoids_marked :
      p1Map.hom.base targetPoint ∉ markedSchemePointSet K) :
    (P1ReductionStep.ofBadValuesComposed
      (K := K) (C := C) (hmarkedOpen := hmarkedOpen) (S := S) (T := T)
      hS badValues hbad aux p1Map hcomposedEtale
      p1Map_maps_bad_to_marked targetPoint maps_T_to_target
      p1Map_target_avoids_marked).composed.hom = aux ≫ p1Map.hom := by
informal_prefix: /-- Belyi reduction layer: the composed reduction step builds its finite Belyi map as `aux` followed by the chosen `P1` Belyi map. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

          exists_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.sendsSetToBranch S φ},
      ∀ x : κ → {x : X // x ∉ S},
        ∃ φ ∈ t,
          x ∈ ((markedNoncriticalExistence K X Φ map continuous_map
            exists_for_finite_disjoint).toBelyiCoverData.complementCoverData S).tupleAvoidSet
              (κ := κ) φ := by

formal_statement:
theorem concrete_polynomial_scheme_separation_exists_concrete_scheme_separation_scalar_package
    (F : Type u) (K : Type v) [Field F] [Field K] [Algebra F K] [IsAlgClosed K]
    {S : Set K} (hS : S.Finite)
    (p : F[X]) (hpder : p.derivative ≠ 0) :
    ∃ β : K, ∃ P : P1PolynomialSeparationStep F K S β,
      P.polynomial = p ∧
        (Polynomial.aeval β P.polynomial ≠ 0 ∧
          Polynomial.aeval β P.polynomial ≠ 1) ∧
          (∀ x ∈ S, Polynomial.aeval x P.polynomial ≠
            Polynomial.aeval β P.polynomial) ∧
            ∀ x : K, Polynomial.aeval x P.polynomial =
              Polynomial.aeval β P.polynomial →
              Polynomial.aeval x P.polynomial.derivative ≠ 0 := by
informal_prefix: /-- Concrete polynomial scheme-separation layer: there is a scalar target value avoiding 0 and 1, separating the finite set, and having only noncritical polynomial preimages. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

    {X P Phi kappa : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace P]
    [Finite kappa] [T1Space P]
    (D : NoncriticalBelyiExistence X P Phi)
    (U : Set X) {S : Set U} (hS : S.Finite)
    [CompactSpace (kappa → {x : U // x ∉ S})] :
    ∃ t : Finset {phi : Phi //
        (D.restrictSubtype U).toBelyiCoverData.sendsSetToBranch S phi},
      (⋃ phi ∈ t,
          ((D.restrictSubtype U).toBelyiCoverData.complementCoverData S).tupleAvoidSet
            (κ := kappa) phi) =
        (Set.univ : Set (kappa → {x : U // x ∉ S})) := by

    {S T : Set C}
    (F : FiniteMarkedBelyiExistence K Phi (P1 K))
    (hS : S.Finite)
    (badValues : Set (P1 K)) (hbad : badValues.Finite)
    (aux : C ⟶ P1 K) [IsFinite aux] [IsDominant aux]
    {targetPoint : P1 K}
    (himage : ∀ x ∈ S, aux.base x ≠ targetPoint)
    (htargetBad : targetPoint ∉ badValues)
    (maps_T_to_target : ∀ x ∈ T, aux.base x = targetPoint)
    (hAuxEtale :
      ∀ phi : Phi,
        ((F.map phi).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set (P1 K)) ⊆
            (reductionBadSet aux S badValues)ᶜ →
          IsEtale (aux ∣_ (F.map phi).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen)) :
    ∃ phi : Phi, ∃ R : P1ReductionStep K C F.hmarkedOpen S T,
      R.p1Map = F.map phi ∧
        R.composed.hom = aux ≫ (F.map phi).hom ∧
          ((∀ x ∈ S, R.composed.hom.base x ∈ markedSchemePointSet K) ∧
            ∀ x ∈ T, R.composed.hom.base x ∉ markedSchemePointSet K) := by

formal_statement:
theorem curve_divisor_sections_divisorZeroSection_exists_p1ReductionAuxiliaryData_of_projectivePair_factory
    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Infinite K] {C : Scheme.{u}}
    {V : Type w} [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    (D : DivisorZeroSectionData K C V)
    {Phi : Type z}
    (F : FiniteMarkedBelyiExistence K Phi (P1 K))
    (hsupport : D.support.Finite) {S : Set C}
    (hdis : Disjoint S D.support)
    (badValues : Set (P1 K)) (hbad : badValues.Finite)
    (mkPair : ∀ s1 : V, HasNoCommonZero D.evalData D.zeroSection s1 →
      ProjectiveLineSectionPair K C V)
    (hmk_eval : ∀ s1 hnc, (mkPair s1 hnc).evalData = D.evalData)
    (hmk_section0 : ∀ s1 hnc, (mkPair s1 hnc).section0 = D.zeroSection)
    (hmk_finite : ∀ s1 hnc, IsFinite (mkPair s1 hnc).hom)
    (hmk_dominant : ∀ s1 hnc, IsDominant (mkPair s1 hnc).hom)
    (htargetBad : schemeCarrierPoint K MarkedPointLabel.zero ∉ badValues)
    (hAuxEtale :
      ∀ s1 hnc phi,
        ((F.map phi).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set (P1 K)) ⊆

      concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K p β ∉
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.SchemeProjectiveLine.markedSchemePointSet K ∧
        (∀ x ∈ S, concreteSchemePolynomialPointMap F K p x ≠
          concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K p β) ∧

            concreteSchemePolynomialTargetPoint F K p β →
            Polynomial.aeval x p.derivative ≠ 0 := by

Use the source-stack theorem. -/

    (A : (j : κ) → Set (Z j))
    (hGA : ∀ e x, x ∈ C e → ∀ j, G (idx e) x j ∈ A j) :
    ∃ H : (j : κ) → Set (Z j),
      (∀ j, IsCompact (H j)) ∧
        (∀ j, H j ⊆ A j) ∧
          ∀ e x, x ∈ C e → ∀ j, G (idx e) x j ∈ H j := by

theorem polynomialMaps_mochizukiPolynomial_derivative_expansion
    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (m n : ℕ) :
    (HilbertTest.SourceStack.PolynomialMaps.mochizukiPolynomial R m n).derivative =
      (Polynomial.C (m : R) * Polynomial.X ^ (m - 1)) *
          ((Polynomial.X : R[X]) - Polynomial.C (1 : R)) ^ n +
        Polynomial.X ^ m *
          (Polynomial.C (n : R) *
            ((Polynomial.X : R[X]) - Polynomial.C (1 : R)) ^ (n - 1)) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 source layer: product-rule expansion for the derivative of x^m (x - 1)^n. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

      Polynomial.X ^ (m - 1) * ((Polynomial.X : R[X]) - Polynomial.C (1 : R)) ^ (n - 1) *
        (Polynomial.C ((m + n : ℕ) : R) * Polynomial.X - Polynomial.C (m : R)) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.1 source layer: the derivative factors as x^(m-1)(x-1)^(n-1)((m+n)x-m). Use the source-stack theorem. -/

header:

universe u w
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_reduction_p1ReductionStep_exists_composedMap_belyiOpen_controls_of_p1MapExistence_auxEtale
    (K : Type u) [Field K] {C : Scheme.{u}} {Phi : Type w}
    {S T : Set C}
    (F : FiniteMarkedBelyiExistence K Phi (P1 K))
    (hS : S.Finite)
    (badValues : Set (P1 K)) (hbad : badValues.Finite)
    (aux : C ⟶ P1 K) [IsFinite aux] [IsDominant aux]
    {targetPoint : P1 K}
    (himage : ∀ x ∈ S, aux.base x ≠ targetPoint)
    (htargetBad : targetPoint ∉ badValues)
    (maps_T_to_target : ∀ x ∈ T, aux.base x = targetPoint)
    (hAuxEtale :
      ∀ phi : Phi,
        ((F.map phi).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set (P1 K)) ⊆
            (reductionBadSet aux S badValues)ᶜ →
          IsEtale (aux ∣_ (F.map phi).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen)) :
    ∃ phi : Phi,
      ∃ composed : FiniteBelyiMap (markedBelyiTarget K F.hmarkedOpen) C,
        composed.hom = aux ≫ (F.map phi).hom ∧
          T ⊆ (composed.toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set C) ∧
            (composed.toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set C) ⊆ Sᶜ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Belyi reduction layer: finite marked Belyi existence on P1 and auxiliary etaleness produce a composed finite Belyi map whose Belyi open contains T and avoids S. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

import HilbertTest.SourceStack.BelyiReduction

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset

universe u
formal_statement:
theorem belyi_reduction_p1ReductionExistence_exists_map_belyiOpen_containing_finite_inside_complement
    (K : Type u) [Field K] {C : Scheme.{u}}
    (E : P1ReductionExistence K C)
    {S T : Set C} (hS : S.Finite) (hT : T.Finite)
    (hdis : Disjoint S T) :
    ∃ i : ReductionIndex C,
      IsOpen ((E.map i).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set C) ∧
        T ⊆ ((E.map i).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set C) ∧
          ((E.map i).toBelyiMap.belyiOpen : Set C) ⊆ Sᶜ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Belyi reduction layer: a global P1-reduction family selects an actual finite Belyi map whose Belyi open contains T and avoids S. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset_avoid_one
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset_avoid_one
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      lam ≠ β - 1 ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, lam ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 strengthened reciprocal-map source layer: choose the pole avoiding β, β-1, and the finite set, while preserving the affine norm separation estimate. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset_avoid_marked
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_projective_reciprocalTranslate_separating_finset_avoid_marked
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      lam ≠ β - 1 ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.one ℂ ∧
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
          (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ β) ≠
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S,
        HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.reciprocalTranslate ℂ lam
            (HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.affinePoint ℂ α) ≠
          HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity ℂ) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 strengthened projective source layer: choose the pole so β maps away from 0, 1, and infinity, finite affine points map away from infinity, and the norm estimate holds. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

    {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq β]

    (hu : u ⊆ s)
    (hucard : u.card = 4)
    (himage : (u.image f).card ≤ 3) :
    ∃ t : Finset β,
      t = s.image f ∧
        (∀ x ∈ s, f x ∈ t) ∧
          t.card < s.card := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set Lemma 2.2 induction handoff: under a four-to-three image hypothesis, the actual image finset is strictly smaller and contains every image. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

    (K : Type u) [CommRing K] [IsDomain K] [T1Space (P1 K)]
    {X : Scheme.{u}}

    (K : Type u) [CommRing K] [IsDomain K]
    (X : Type v) [TopologicalSpace X] (Φ : Type w) (κ : Type*)
    (map : Φ → X → _root_.ProjectiveSpectrum (grading K))
    (continuous_map : ∀ φ, Continuous (map φ))
    (exists_for_finite_disjoint :
      ∀ {S T : Set X}, S.Finite → T.Finite → Disjoint S T →
        ∃ φ : Φ, (∀ x ∈ S, map φ x ∈ markedPointSet K) ∧
          ∀ x ∈ T, map φ x ∉ markedPointSet K)
    [Finite κ] [T1Space (_root_.ProjectiveSpectrum (grading K))]
    {S : Set X} (hS : S.Finite) [CompactSpace (κ → {x : X // x ∉ S})] :
    ∃ t : Finset {φ : Φ //
        (markedNoncriticalExistence K X Φ map continuous_map
